title: OR defect cancellation and tangentiality
summary: Expresses the boundary defect of an OR-indexed operator in a finite obstruction basis, converts rational cancellation to real cancellation, and proves conditional bidifferential tangentiality.

mathematicalContent.heading: Defect expansion
mathematicalContent.body: For homogeneous, boundary-equivalent input pairs F,G, an interface identifies P_D(F)−P_D(G) modulo Q with a finite obstruction sum.

mathematicalContent.heading: Rational-to-real cancellation
mathematicalContent.body: If real defect coefficients are casts of rational coefficients, a rational zero-sum proof casts to the real zero-sum needed by the ambient operator.

mathematicalContent.heading: Tangentiality
mathematicalContent.body: Once every coefficient vanishes, the defect sum is zero and the operator has the same boundary value on equivalent homogeneous extensions.

correspondence.leanType: structure ORDefectExpansion {κ : Type _} [DecidableEq κ] (D : OROperatorData) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) (K : Finset κ)
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Stores an OR-indexed boundary-defect expansion in a finite basis of bidifferential obstruction operators.

correspondence.leanType: def SatisfiesDefectCancellation {κ : Type _} [DecidableEq κ] (D : OROperatorData) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) {K : Finset κ} (E : D.ORDefectExpansion CalConf K) : Prop
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines real defect cancellation by vanishing of every obstruction coefficient.

correspondence.leanType: def SatisfiesRationalDefectCancellation {κ : Type _} [DecidableEq κ] (D : OROperatorData) (K : Finset κ) (defectCoeffQ : ORIndex D.N → Fin 2 → κ → ℚ) : Prop
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines rational defect cancellation using the rational coefficient-system predicate.

correspondence.leanType: theorem satisfiesDefectCancellation_of_rational {κ : Type _} [DecidableEq κ] (D : OROperatorData) {K : Finset κ} (E : D.ORDefectExpansion CalConf K) (defectCoeffQ : ORIndex D.N → Fin 2 → κ → ℚ) (hE : ∀ (I : ORIndex D.N) (slot : Fin 2) (k : κ), E.defectCoeffR I slot k = (defectCoeffQ I slot k : ℝ)) (hQ : D.SatisfiesRationalDefectCancellation K defectCoeffQ) : D.SatisfiesDefectCancellation CalConf E
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Casts a rational cancellation proof to real cancellation when the expansion coefficients agree with the rational model.

correspondence.leanType: theorem isBiTangential_of_defect_cancellation {κ : Type _} [DecidableEq κ] (D : OROperatorData) {K : Finset κ} (E : D.ORDefectExpansion CalConf K) (hcancel : D.SatisfiesDefectCancellation CalConf E) : CalConf.IsBiTangentialAtWeights (D.toOperator CalConf) (D.w₁ : ℝ) (D.w₂ : ℝ)
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves bidifferential tangentiality from the supplied real defect expansion and its cancellation equations.

correspondence.leanType: theorem isBiTangential_of_rational_defect_cancellation {κ : Type _} [DecidableEq κ] (D : OROperatorData) {K : Finset κ} (E : D.ORDefectExpansion CalConf K) (defectCoeffQ : ORIndex D.N → Fin 2 → κ → ℚ) (hE : ∀ (I : ORIndex D.N) (slot : Fin 2) (k : κ), E.defectCoeffR I slot k = (defectCoeffQ I slot k : ℝ)) (hQ : D.SatisfiesRationalDefectCancellation K defectCoeffQ) : CalConf.IsBiTangentialAtWeights (D.toOperator CalConf) (D.w₁ : ℝ) (D.w₂ : ℝ)
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Combines rational cancellation and coefficient compatibility to obtain bidifferential tangentiality.

correspondence.leanType: def toWeightedBiTangentialOperator (D : OROperatorData) (hmaps : CalConf.MapsWeights (D.toOperator CalConf) (D.inputWeights CalConf) D.outputWeight) (htangential : CalConf.IsBiTangentialAtWeights (D.toOperator CalConf) (D.w₁ : ℝ) (D.w₂ : ℝ)) : CalConf.WeightedBiTangentialOperator
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Packages independently proved weight mapping and tangentiality as a weighted bidifferential operator.

references.locator: Classification of invariant bilinear differential operators
references.note: Flat-model origin of the coefficient family.
Primary curved ambient source for the encoded OR recurrence.

limitations: The decisive combination_defect identity is a field of ORDefectExpansion and is not derived here from ambient commutators. Rational cancellation is also a supplied hypothesis; the theorem proves the logical passage from those inputs to tangentiality.
